bullets: Run-rate $10.60B in FY2026 Q1, -3.5% YoY — contracting trajectory entering the next print.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $44B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $42B → FY2 $45B (+6.4% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +10.1% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 105.2% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 0.59 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±17.0% ($167.07 – $235.39) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: +1.29pp — put skew (downside hedges bid).

short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $191 (5% OTM). High IV (105%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 57.7% across 9 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 9 dates spanning 98–616 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±33.4% ($134.10 – $268.36).

long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $181 put / short $221 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
